title: Impatiens superglabra
taxonomy: Impatiens superglabra is a species of flowering plant in the balsam family, Balsamaceae. It is native to tropical Africa. As of my knowledge cutoff, Impatiens superglabra is a part of the genus Impatiens, which is a group of plants commonly known as snapweed or jewelweed. The genus Impatiens is typically classified in the family Balsamaceae, although some sources may group it in the family Dipterocarpaceae. However, the exact classification of Impatiens superglabra may vary depending on the source.

intro: Impatiens superba, also known as jewelweed or touch-me-not, is a flowering plant native to North America, characterized by its bright orange, yellow, or red flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with health benefits including the treatment of skin irritations and insect bites due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Impatiens superba include lawsone, a yellow pigment with antimicrobial and antioxidant activities, as well as other flavonoids and phenolic acids. In herbal medicine, the plant is often made into a salve or tincture to treat skin conditions, wounds, and insect bites.
